fix.item: Fix bug with ajax request in Joomla 5

changelog.addition: Option to limit amount of reviews to be displayed
Option to display reviews by newest or random.
Option to hide reviews without text.

fix.item: Added missing license in php file

addition.item: Option to limit amount of reviews to be displayed
Option to display reviews by newest or random.
Option to hide reviews without text.

fix.item: Made the module fetching more robust with fallback
Language constant for "View all reviews" instead of hardcoded Dutch. Ouch!

addition.item: Four layout templates: default (carousel), card-carousel, list, and compact (sidebar/footer).
Legacy layout preserved as tmpl/legacy.php for users with existing template overrides.
Display settings tab: autoplay toggle, show/hide rating summary, review count, profile photos, review date, "View all reviews" button.
Review text truncation: configurable maximum character limit (default 250).
Load layout CSS toggle: opt-out of the built-in stylesheet per module instance.
CSS grid stacking fix for carousel height jumping between slides of different text lengths.
Layout field moved from Advanced tab to Display tab in module settings.
Empty-state language string (MOD_PRETTYREVIEWS_NO_REVIEWS) shown when no reviews are available.

security.item: AJAX refresh endpoint now requires the Joomla CSRF form token (POST) and a per-module core.edit permission check.
Google API key, Place ID and review-sort are no longer rendered as data-* attributes or sent in the request URL — credentials are read server-side from the module record.
Replaced raw cURL with HttpFactory; TLS certificate verification is now always on.
SQL in the module-lookup fallback now uses bound parameters and quoteName().
All review fields are HTML-escaped on output; profile photo and author URLs are validated to use http(s) schemes.

change.item: Removed the unused "secret" field from the module parameters (breaking: existing installs simply ignore the stale value).
Helper API split into loadRaw / present / refreshFromGoogle. Cache files no longer overwritten with a filtered subset on every refresh.
Admin refresh now returns clean JSON feedback and reports Google Places API errors instead of treating empty or rejected responses as successful updates.
